metadata.title: WRITTEN SUBMISSIONS TO THE JOINT SELECT COMMITTEE ON THE DRAFT ONLINE SAFETY BILL
metadata.author: download (34)

chunks.text: Thank you for the opportunity to provide input to the Joint Select Committee regarding the draft Online Safety Bill. The proposed Bill is a positive step towards introducing comprehensive legislation that improves online safety in the UK. As the leading British social media company, OnlyFans is building the safest online digital media platform in the world, and so we welcome the constructive approach the Government and this Committee are taking to ensure the Bill enshrines clear legal standards to protect individuals online, whilst ensuring creativity and innovation continue to flourish in the UK. We therefore wholeheartedly support the Bill. We believe that creativity, freedom of expression, and connectivity online are at their best when people are interacting in a safe environment. Unlike many other social media platforms, OnlyFans already has put in place many of the policies and procedures proposed in the Bill and we welcome the level playing field that the Bill will create. Our approach to online safety is different from other social media because of the way we built the platform and the user experience. In particular: • OnlyFans is only accessible to users and creators whoareover the age of 18 and we have robust procedures in place to keep people who are under 18 off the OnlyFans platform. • OnlyFans has a comprehensive identity verification process for our users, unlike other social media platforms which are open to the public and beset by anonymous members. • OnlyFans operates on a subscription model, sothevast majority of our content is behind a paywall. 100 percent of the sexually explicit content on OnlyFans is behind a paywall and because of this, users cannot stumble across sexually explicit images liketheycan on other platforms. • OnlyFans’ subscription-based model means that content published exclusively on OnlyFans does not "go viral" in contrast to other social media platforms. • OnlyFans does not have a traditional search function unlike other social media platforms that allow a user to search for a topic across an entire platform. Fans find creators on other platforms (e. g., an athlete, TV star, or influencer) and then choose to also follow them on OnlyFans. • OnlyFans limits communications between creators and their fans. Fans cannot connect with creators without first having subscribed to, and engaged with, their content. This radically reduces cyberbullying, trolling, and the ability of internet mobstogangup on someone. • OnlyFans can screen direct messages between users on the platform. This means that if someone does be have unlawfully, inappropriately, orinanyway which violates our Terms of Service and/or Acceptable Use Policy , (a copy of our current Terms of Service and Acceptable Use Policy can be found at Annex 1) in direct messages, we can determine who is behind the messages and take appropriate action against that individual. • OnlyFans does not independently monetise our fans’ or creators’ dataforourown benefit, unlike other social media platforms. Creators’ content and their data belong to them. We donotsellany fan or creator data to third-party advertisers.

Creators’ content and their data belong to them. We donotsellany fan or creator data to third-party advertisers. Our users have autonomy over the composition of their newsfeeds and the content they choose to view. • OnlyFans utilises a comprehensive blend of state-of-the-art technologies and extensive human, manual review of content in fur the rance of our mission to be the safest platform in the world. All content on the platform is screened with in 24 hours of being posted by our trained team of content reviewers, whomakeupmore than 80 percent of our employees. Additionally, all creators are reverified on a rolling cycle every 30 days. This process is described in detail below. For all of these reasons, andmanymore, OnlyFans supports the introduction of the Bill. OnlyFans believes that social media platformscan, and should, be a safe space which also allow creativity to flourish, and we believe the ways in which we differ from other social media platforms support these objectives. We believe in creating a level playing field between all websites, regardless of their business model. A uniform approach, as proposed in the Bill, would ensure that all creators and users enjoy thesamehigh level of protection and that allplat for ms on which such content is hosted are subject to the same stringent requirements and compliance obligations. OnlyFans and our business model To understand OnlyFans' perspective on the Bill, it is important to understand the OnlyFans platform and our approach to online safety. OnlyFans was established in the UK in 2016 by our British founder and CEO Tim Stokely to provide a new path for content creators. At the time, creators had two options to make money on their content: they could either share their content forfreeandhope that advertising or promotional revenue would follow, or they could try to catch the attention of an intermediary such as an agent or a publisher who would pay them for their content. OnlyFans provided creators with a third waythatgave people control over their own content and revolutionised creator and fan connections by creating a platform that allows fans to subscribe directly to a creator's content and connect personally with the creator in the process. As a result, OnlyFans is a unique and inclusive subscription-based platform that gives creators from all genres the ability to connect directly with their fans, monetise their content, and own their intellectual property. Our business model is simple and transparent. Unlike the traditional entertainment industry, OnlyFans gives creators meaningful, lucrative creative control and agency. Unlike other social media sites, we do not allow third-party adsorseek to monetise our fans’ and creators' personal datainanyway. We do all this while building the safest online digital media platform in the world. We do all this while building the safest online digital media platform in the world. Since its creation, over two million verified creators have joined OnlyFans to share their creative content with over This remarkable growth has made OnlyFans a British social media “unicorn” and is representative of the UK's enormous contribution to global innovation at the confluence of the entertainment and tech industries. Our creators include artists, musicians, actors, influencers, chefs, athletes, photographers, adult creators, and many other genres. It is a fundamental misconception that OnlyFans is a platform which was created for, and focuses solely on, sexually explicit content. We are proud of the diversity of our creator community and we foster an inclusive and lawful approach to social media and entertainment. We work extremely hard to support our diverse community and to ensure everyone feels safe and respected on our platform. OnlyFans is only accessible to users whoareover the age of 18. Fans identify creators they want to follow – across all genres and through other traditional media and social media platforms – and then choose to also follow them on OnlyFans. Thefanscanthen elect to pay to subscribe to the creator’s exclusive content posted on OnlyFans. This empowers individuals working in creative industries to fight back against the idea of “free content” and provides a platform for them to monetise their creative output. As Australian indie pop musician Holiday Sidewinder noted in Slate, “ I'm lucky enough tohaveavery loyal fan base … I didn'twanttofeel like a charity. OnlyFans seemed to capture what I actually wanted, which was a place for my fans, subscription-based, where I could get paid to provide the content that I provide anyway .” On OnlyFans, artists and musicians like Ms. Sidewinder share unreleased demos, retail discounts, and insights in to their creative process, building authentic relationships with their fans. In turn, this enables fans to foster authentic online connections with creators, from global celebrities like musician Cardi B and NFL star Terrell Owens to their local yoga instructor, favourite chef, or personal trainer. OnlyFans empowers individuals from around the world to use their unique talents and creativity to develop and share content with audiences, newandold alike. Fitness trainer Jono Castano told the Daily Mail Online “ I wanted to offer my daily workouts to everyone on more of a global level. OnlyFans hasexpanded enormously during the pandemic and fitness has become oneofthetop-performing categories on the platform. Now, I'm able to further reach people all over the world — I'm getting subscribers fromsomany countries, most of which I may have not reached without the platform .” OnlyFans also empowers organisations. Organisations which foster and promote creative content are now turning to OnlyFans innewways that demonstrate the powerful and innovative nature of our platform. Just one example is the city of Vienna's tourism board, which recently opened an OnlyFans account “ in protest against platforms' ongoing censorship of Peterson, "Celebrity personal trainer Jono Castano reveals how using OnlyFans has helped expand his fitness business during the Covid pandemic", Daily Mail Online , 16 September 2021, https://bityl. co/98wV museums and galleries.” By way of background, traditional social media platformshad censored certain classic works of art posted by a number of museums in Vienna. Specifically, the Albertina Museum's account was suspended and blocked on one social media platform for showing the works of Japanese photographer Nobuyoshi Araki. Additionally in 2018, the Natural History Museum's photograph of the 25,000-year-old Venus of Willendorf figurine was deemed pornographic and removed by another platform. Finally, the Leopold Museum has also struggled to promote the expressionist Egon Schiele and a painting by Koloman Moser. Thisartnow is on full worldwide display on OnlyFans, safe from the threat of cultural censorship. We are proud that these traditional works of art, and more, are now available in a new and innovative wayandcan reach new global audiences. Helena Hartlauer, a Vienna tourist board spokesperson, told The Guardian , “ Of course you can work without that, but these artworks are crucial and important to Vienna – when you think of the self-portrait by Schiele from 1910, it's oneofthemost iconic artworks. If they cannot be used on a communications tool as strong as social media, it's unfair and frustrating. That's why we thought [of OnlyFans]: finally, a waytoshow these things.” The Vienna tourism board’s example highlights how our platform canbeused in diverse and innovative ways. We will always strive to have the safest platform in the world and are just scratching the surface of the opportunities the platform has to offer. OnlyFans has become a source of innovation, powered by organic ideas that are reaching new audiences and markets, while providing protection to freedom of expression. We are excited about the future of OnlyFans and about the unlimited possibilities ahead. As these examples demonstrate, our unique approach supports the creative economy and allows creators of all genres to develop authentic relationships with their followers and fans, whilst empowering them to maximise control over and monetise their content. OnlyFans provides a platform to enable authentic connections between creators and fans. While this is a technical and labour-intensive process, it is key to our safety program and we are committed to making this investment in protecting fans and creators on our platform. OnlyFans also requires all fans to provide their name and payment card details to access content behind the paywall. By requiring a payment card, we leverage the entire payments ecosystem to confirm fan identities and validate that they are over the age of 18. In addition to requiring that all users submit a payment card, OnlyFans has also implemented automated age-verification in certain jurisdictions - where users may be required to use their phone’s camera apptoscan their face, tomakesure they are clearly over 18 years of age, otherwise they will need to provide their photo ID as proof of their age. Assetout in our Transparency Reports from July to September 2021, over 60 percent of applications were rejected because they failed to meet the requirements of the account verification and vetting process, demonstrating the rigour with which OnlyFans approaches our onboarding process and our fidelity to policies and procedures that were carefully developed to protect community members. Creators who are successfully verified are a ssigned a risk score based on the user verification in for mation submitted. We use that score to identify higher risk creators who require additional follow-up checks to validate compliance with our policies and procedures. In addition, on a rolling 30-day cycle, creators are subject to secondary "selfie checks". This means that our content moderation teamperforms additional cross checks on creator accounts to ensure that the person(s) featured in any content posted on OnlyFans are a) appropriately tagged, and b) match the verified identification documents provided when the account was opened. This is to ensure that after an account is successfully created, the account details are not given to, or used by, an unverified user. Where we identify a person featured in content who is not the listed account owner/creator, our analysts ensure that all subjects are tagged as verified creators. Where this is not the case, steps are taken to ensure that the additional subject is added as a creator and is then subject to the same creator identity verification steps outlined above. Any content which is posted without proper tagging of multiple subjects is suspended until all creators have been tagged and verified in keeping with our Terms of Service and Acceptable Use Policy and we obtain the necessary release and consent forms for any third parties

In keeping with the Bill's provisions, OnlyFans believes that there is content which is not illegal, but which can still be harmful to users. OnlyFans does not allow what it deems to be harmful content on our platform. This content includes, for example: images or videos involving injury or risk of injury, blood, or medical procedures. Harmful content is removed, creators who post harmful content are warned that they are in breach of the Terms of Service and should there be any further violations, the account is deactivated. Details of the content that is, and is not permitted, on OnlyFans is set out clearly in our Acceptable Use Policy and can be found at Annex 1 (see pages 29 – 31, paragraphs 5a-j, 6 and 7 of the Terms of Service and Acceptable Use Policy). OnlyFans utilises a comprehensive blend of state-of-the-art technologies and extensive human manual review of content. Ultimately, all content on the platform is screened with in 24 hours of being posted by our trained team of individual, manual content reviewers. It is then further reviewed by additional content moderation as detailed below. Prior to publication, OnlyFans also reviews content for words which OnlyFans has identified as being a ssociated with illegal and/or harmful content and/or content with a hash value associated with illegal content (a hash is a sequence of letters and numbers which acts a unique identifier similar to a “digital fingerprint”). Any content which contains words associated with illegal and/or harmful content or known “bad” hashes is blocked at the outset, prior to being posted on OnlyFans. All content is ticketed for detailed review, with in 24 hours, by hundreds of analysts trained to conduct manual content moderation. With in seconds, content is run through multiple third-party tools, some of which are powered by artificial intelligence to detect adult content, minors, offensive content, multiple subjects, weapons, violent content, and alcohol, and flagged for human review. Content is also run through automated text detection tools for prohibited words or phrases. Any content which is flagged by our A I toolisthen escalated to the 24-hour content moderation team. The content moderation teamismadeup of approximately 750 trained manual reviewers who are involved in some element of content moderation including anti-fraud, verification, support, and compliance with the Digital Millennium Copyright Act ("DMCA", the United States federal copyright law which demands that third-party sites enforce our creators’ proprietary rights by removing infringing materials.) These reviewers currently makeupmorethan 80 percent of all OnlyFans employees, demonstrating our commitment to effective content moderation and we continue to invest heavily

We follow NCMEC guidelines andstandard operating procedures on reporting, particularly ifthesame content is identified and associated with a user with multiple accounts, we only report that matter once. However, if we identify a prohibited piece of content inmorethanone channel, we may report thateachtime to NCMEC. We quarantine and preserve these accounts in a secure server, should evidence be required forlawen for cement enquiries. From July to September 2021, 131 pieces of media were reported to the NCMEC CyberTipline by OnlyFans. From January to June 2021, YouTube made 124,773 reports to NCMEC and Google made 287,368 reports to NCMEC. When we identify new CSAM, where necessary or appropriate, we calculate a hash of the content , add that in for mation to our internal repository, and report it to NCMEC and other third-party tools. Hashing technology allows us, and others, to find previously identified CSAM. We also share hash values with other providers, sotheycan access these hashes as well. In addition, we work with third parties to identify and share IP addresses associated with CSAM content. Contributing to NCMEC's and others’ hash databases is one of the critical ways to fight illegal content online. In September, OnlyFans proactively provided 106 reports and 36 new hashes to the CyberTipline. OnlyFans welcomes the introduction of a "dutyofcare" on Category 1 services to protect adults from "harmful" content under clause 11 of the Bill. It will be crucial to ensure this is carefully defined andenforced equally across all It is imperative that the outlines ofsuchfar-reaching changes to existing content moderation obligations aresetout clearly in primary legislation passed by Parliament as opposed to being left to secondary legislation or the Courts, which would create high levels of uncertainty. OnlyFans supports the Government'sefforts to define "harmful" content. As it is presently drafted, the Bill requires that a service provider identifies content that poses " a material risk of the content having, or indirectly having, a significant adverse physical or psychological impact on an adult of ordinary sensibilities " (see clause 46(3)) or content which presents " a material riskofthefact of the content's dissemination having a significant adverse physical or psychological impact on an adult of ordinary sensibilities, taking in to account (in particular) – how many users may be a ssumed to encounter the content by means of the service and how easily, quickly and widely content may be disseminated by means of the service " (see clause 46(5)). The Bill does not further define the term " an adult of ordinary sensibilities ". We recognise that it will be difficult to reach consensus of what constitutes "harmful" content as this is likely to differ greatly from person to person, especially in the realms of adult and sexually explicit content between consenting adults. (As discussed above, we already prohibit a substantial amount of content that would likely be characterised as harmful, such as content that promotes or displays violence, intoxication, drug-use, and firearms.) It may also diminish the safe space which OnlyFans currently offers to adult content creators, whoareable to share and monetise their content in a secure environment, in which fans and creators alike are identity verified. We welcome ongoing discussion with the Committee and Ofcom. Further, any uncertainty could play in to concerns relating to freedom of expression. Any ambiguity in the standardsmay lead social media platforms and other websites to errontheside of caution and there by potentially stifle the ability of creators to express themselves freely and safely online. OnlyFans restricts access to individuals whoareover 18 years old. For the avoidance of doubt, OnlyFans supports the introduction of a dutyofcare in respect of illegal content and for "harmful content" in relation to services likely to be a ccessed by children. Is Ofcom suitable for and capable of undertaking the role proposed for it in the draft Bill? As Ofcom currently regulates video sharing platforms ("VSPs"), it is best placed to regulate the implementation of the Bill. Given the wide scope of the Bill and the novel are as it covers, OnlyFans supports additional funding for Ofcom to ensure it can meet its regulatory responsibilities under the Bill. OnlyFans looks forward to continuing constructive engagement with Ofcom. Additional

Category 1 designation OnlyFans supports the Bill's proposition to designate Category 1 services. Further refinement of this categorisation may be helpful to ensure that the right social media plat for msanduser-to-user services are caught and to reduce the compliance burden on smaller organisations who may not present thesamerisk as larger social media platforms, with users who are a dults and children. This would ensure a proportionate approach which accurately captures the differing architecture, size, and impact of user-to-user services. From a policy st and point, there may be merit in reflecting the impact of different business models on online safety in the categorisation. This would certainly drive greater proactive adoption of online safety measures and the implementation of best practices around user verification and other safeguards. Rewarding businesses who are “safer by design” reflects a nuanced, innovative, and business friendly approach to regulation. Additional compliance burden Due to our desire to be the safest platform in the world, OnlyFans is a lready materially compliant with many provisions of the Bill. Through our market-leading user onboarding and content moderation practices and policies described above, we ensure that OnlyFans is on track to meet our obligations under the Bill, once it enters in to force. However, we also agree with the submissions made to this Committee by SnapInc. and Reddit that the additional compliance burden the Bill imposes on smaller organisations may be substantial. This may discourage new entrants to the market, particularly among the emerging British technology companies, which may negatively affect competition and increase the dominance of the existing "BigTech" platforms. Other adult content websites OnlyFans is an inclusive platform of creators from all genres, including adult creators, who can monetise their own content. As a result of our business model which is based on peer-to-peer sharing, OnlyFans is subject to the Bill. Other adult content websites that do not empower creators inthesameway fall outside the current draft of the Bill, because they do not qualify as user-generated content services. OnlyFans believes in creating a level playing field between all websites, regardless of their business model. A uniform approach would ensure that creators and users across allsuchplat for ms enjoy the same protections and that theplatforms on which such content is hosted are subject to the same stringent requirements and compliance obligations. Conclusion OnlyFans believes that social media platformscan, and should, be a safe space which also allows creativity to flourish.
